zh.Brampton, Devon, England: 英國英格蘭戴文布勞頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Cogan於1299年10月9日出生在英國戴文巴普頓時，他的父親Thomas de Cogan23歲，母親Petronilla Parnell14歲。他於1327年5月10日與Lady Mary de Montagu在英國英格蘭薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1368年4月20日在他的家鄉享年68歲，埋葬在英國戴文巴普頓。

zh-hans.Brampton, Devon, England: 英国英格兰戴文布勞頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Cogan于1299年10月9日出生在英国戴文巴普頓时，他的父亲Thomas de Cogan23岁，母亲Petronilla Parnell14岁。他于1327年5月10日与Lady Mary de Montagu在英国英格兰薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1368年4月20日在他的家乡享年68岁，埋葬在英国戴文巴普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Cogan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish (from Ukraine) Kogan, from the Russian pronunciation of Cohen .

Irish and Welsh (of Norman origin): habitational name from Cogan near Cardiff, which may have been named with a Welsh word meaning ‘bowl, depression’. In 1166 Milo de Cogan, held two fees near Penarth (Glamorganshire) of William earl of Gloucester. Members of this Norman family probably account for the name's appearance in 13th-century Berwickshire. By this time the family was also present in Ireland, associated with counties Limerick and Cork. It may have been the Irish branch that later brought the name to southwest Scotland.

variants.name: Cogen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Dorith.  German Erwin.

variants.name: Cohen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Isadore, Emanuel, Moshe, Shlomo, Myer, Avi, Yehuda, Chaim.
Jewish: from Hebrew kohen ‘priest’. Priests are traditionally regarded as members of a hereditary caste descended from Aaron, brother of Moses. See also Kaplan .
Irish (Galway and Sligo): variant of   Coyne  .

variants.name: Coggins
meanings: English: variant of Cogan , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Coggan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brigid, Conal, Seamus.  Jewish Hyman.
Americanized form of Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic) Kogan, from the Russian pronunciation of Cohen .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Cogan was born on 9 October 1299, in Bampton, Devon, England, his father, Thomas de Cogan, was 23 and his mother, Petronilla Parnell, was 14. He married Lady Mary de Montagu on 10 May 1327, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 20 April 1368, in his hometown, at the age of 68, and was buried in Bampton, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Cogan est né le 9 octobre 1299, à Bampton, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Cogan, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Petronilla Parnell, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Lady Mary de Montagu le 10 mai 1327, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 20 avril 1368, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Bampton, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Cogan am 9. Oktober 1299 in Bampton, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Cogan 23 und seine Mutter Petronilla Parnell 14. Er hat am 10. Mai 1327 in Somerset, England, United Kingdom Lady Mary de Montagu geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 20. April 1368 im Alter von 68 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Bampton, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Cogan è nato il il 9 ottobre 1299 nel Bampton, Devon, England, il suo padre, Thomas de Cogan, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Petronilla Parnell, aveva 14 anni. Lui sposato Lady Mary de Montagu il 10 maggio 1327, nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 20 aprile 1368, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a Bampton, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coganが1299年10月9日にイギリスデボンバンプトンで生まれた時，父Thomas de Coganは23歳，母Petronilla Parnellは14歳であった。1327年5月10日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトでLady Mary de Montaguと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1368年4月20日に故郷で68歳で死去し，イギリスデボンバンプトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Cogan(이)가 1299년 10월 9일 영국 데폰 바프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Cogan(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Petronilla Parnell(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1327년 5월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 Lady Mary de Montagu(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1368년 4월 20일 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 바프턴에 묻혔다.

pt.9 October 1299–20 April 1368: 9 de outubro de 1299–20 de abril de 1368
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Cogan nasceu em 9 de outubro de 1299, em Bampton, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Cogan, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Petronilla Parnell, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Mary de Montagu em 10 de maio de 1327, em Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 20 de abril de 1368, em sua cidade natal, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Bampton, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Cogan родился 9 октября 1299 г. в местности бамптон, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas de Cogan было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Petronilla Parnell, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Mary de Montagu 10 мая 1327 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 20 апреля 1368 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности бамптон, девон, Англия.

es.9 October 1299–20 April 1368: 9 de octubre de 1299–20 de abril de 1368
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Cogan nació el 9 de octubre de 1299, en Bampton, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Cogan, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Petronilla Parnell, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Lady Mary de Montagu el 10 de mayo de 1327, en Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 20 de abril de 1368, en su pueblo, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Bampton, Devon, Inglaterra.
